title: [lora_l2] tx_buf_mutex not reinitialized in deinit
description:
Cross-file race condition: lichen_lora_l2_deinit() reinitializes lora_mutex (line 575) but NOT tx_buf_mutex.

If the module is deinitialized while a TX is in progress (see issue about TX in flight during deinit), and tx_buf_mutex was left locked by crashed/aborted code, subsequent init()+start()+tx() calls would deadlock on the corrupted tx_buf_mutex.

The scenario:
1. TX thread holds tx_buf_mutex, calls lora_send()
2. System issue causes need to deinit
3. deinit() called - reinitializes lora_mutex but not tx_buf_mutex
4. tx_buf_mutex is left in locked state (or corrupted)
5. Module reinitialized via init()
6. Next TX call blocks forever on tx_buf_mutex

This parallels the existing handling where deinit() reinitializes lora_mutex for the same reason (to recover from aborted state). tx_buf_mutex should receive the same treatment.

Location: lora_l2.c:575 (should also reinitialize tx_buf_mutex)
close_reason: Fixed: tx_buf_mutex now reinitialized in deinit() alongside lora_mutex
